text: Patient is a 63-year-old male chronic smoker with a positive family history of lung cancer.
CT scan thorax was suggestive of 1.4 x 1.2 cm mixed GGO in the left upper lobe anterior segment.
We started with a standard 3-port WADS approach, dissecting the hilum first to skeletonize left superior pulmonary vein.
V3A was identified, dissected and divided after suture ligation at the proximal end and harmonic distally.
Surrounding media channel lymph nodes dissected.
A3 identified and divided with staplers.
anterior segment bronchus deeper to the eye tree, dissected and divided using staplers.
V4 plus V5 draining to the lingula was safeguarded.
S3 segment demarcated, preserving adjacent segmental veins.
Anterior segmentectomy was completed using staplers.
Post-operative period was uneventful, the lung was completely expanded, intercostal drain was removed on third day and the patient was discharged.
